texto: How much truth is contained in something can be best determined by making it thoroughly laughable and then watching to see how much joking around it can take. For truth is a matter that can withstand mockery, that is freshened by any ironic gesture directed at it. Whatever cannot withstand satire is false.
explicacao: Citação de Peter Sloterdijk sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “How much truth is contained in something can be best determined by making it thoroughly laughable…” articula reflexao, irony, parody em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como parody e reasoning.

texto: As in the days of the first Merovingian, who pledged allegiance to the cross because of a victorious battle, today's children of the banalized Enlightenment are likewise meant to burn what they worshipped and worship what they burned.

texto: [Nietzsche's] questions - transcend, but where to; ascend, but to what height? - would have answered themselves if he had calmly kept both feet on the ascetic ground. He was too sick to follow his most important insight: that the main thing in life is to take the minor things seriously. When minor things grow stronger, the danger posed by the main thing is contained; then climbing higher in the minor things means advancing in the main thing.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Peter Sloterdijk que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “[Nietzsche's] questions - transcend, but where to; ascend, but to what height? - would have answe…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, asceticism, ascetics, com foco em termino e ascetics.

texto: In the midst of the ubiquitous dealings with prostituted signs, the thing-poem was capable of opening up the prospect of returning to credible experiences of meaning. It did this by tying language to the gold standard of what things themselves communicate. Where randomness is disabled, authority should shine forth.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Peter Sloterdijk sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “In the midst of the ubiquitous dealings with prostituted signs, the thing-poem was capable of ope…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, authority, being, útil em momentos de being e communication.

texto: What early Christianity meant by 'faith' (pistis) was initially nothing other than running ahead and clinging to a model or idea whose attainability was still uncertain. Faith is purely anticipatory, in the sense that it already has an effect when it mobilizes the existence of the anticipatory towards the goal through anticipation. In analogy for the placebo effect, one would have to call this the movebo effect.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Peter Sloterdijk sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “What early Christianity meant by 'faith' (pistis) was initially nothing other than running ahead …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, anthropology, anticipation, útil em momentos de anticipation e faith.

texto: In his field, and with his means, Rilke carries out an operation that one could philosophically describe as the 'transformation of being into message' (more commonly, 'linguistic turn'). 'Being that can be be understood is language', Heidegger would later state - which conversely implies that language abandoned by being becomes mere chatter.

texto: Wherever one encounters members of the human race, they always show the traits of a being that is condemned to surrealistic effort. Whoever goes in search of humans will find acrobats.

texto: The reason for the existence of the perfection conjured up in these fourteen lines is that it possesses ... the authorization to form a message that appeals from within itself. This power of appeal is exquisitely evident in the object evoked here. The perfect thing is that which articulates an entire principle of being. The poem has to perform no more and no less than to perceive the principle of being in the thing and adapt it to its own existence - with the aim of becoming a construct with an equal power to convey a message.
explicacao: Citação de Peter Sloterdijk sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The reason for the existence of the perfection conjured up in these fourteen lines is that it pos…” articula reflexao, authority, being em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como being e poetry.
